quote: "The history of every day. -- What is the history of every day in your case? Look at your habits that constitute it: are they the product of innumerable little cowardices and lazinesses, or of your courage and inventive reason?"
intro: On the record of your days
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
explanation: Philosopher Friedrich Nietzsche on how your daily habits become the true history of your life

quote: "I am Me, and I hope to become Me more and more."
author: Paula Modersohn-Becker
explanation: Painter Paula Modersohn-Becker on selfhood as a continual process of becoming

quote: "A dead thing can go with the stream, but only a living thing can go against it."
author: G. K. Chesterton
explanation: Writer G. K. Chesterton on living with energy, will, and conviction rather than being carried along by life

quote: "The truest form of intelligence is designing the life you want to live."
author: Graham Weaver
explanation: Founder and investor Graham Weaver on what it truly means to be intelligent

quote: "Always play the hand you have, not the one you wish you had."
author: Garry Tan
explanation: Entrepreneur and investor Garry Tan on working with your current reality rather than wishing for a different one

quote: "If you want to get warm you must stand near the fire: if you want to be wet you must get into the water. If you want joy, power, peace, eternal life, you must get close to, or even into, the thing that has them."
author: C. S. Lewis
explanation: Writer and scholar C. S. Lewis on how skills are acquired through direct participation, not at a distance

quote: "Go at it boldly, and you'll find unexpected forces closing round you and coming to your aid."
intro: On the power of decisive action
author: Basil King
explanation: Clergyman and writer Basil King on how bold action attracts support from unexpected sources

quote: "Obsessions are the only things that matter."
author: Patricia Highsmith
explanation: Novelist Patricia Highsmith on how results come from sustained focus, not casual interest

author: Mirko Ilić
explanation: Comic artist and graphic designer Mirko Ilić on saving time by thinking deeply before acting

quote: "Where is your Self to be found? Always in the deepest enchantment that you have experienced."
author: Hugo von Hofmannsthal
explanation: Poet and playwright Hugo von Hofmannsthal on discovering your true self in moments of deep enchantment
